summary: PowerShell code quality is solid (Set-StrictMode, error handling, parameter validation, -LiteralPath, StringBuilder), but deliverable is critically incomplete: (1) `SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1` test file is missing entirely despite being referenced in workflow line 19 and required by task; (2) no test fixtures (mock commits.txt, package.json) provided; (3) `actions/checkout@v4` not pinned to SHA (best practice violation; task explicitly requires actionlint); (4) workflow_dispatch input `release_date` wired but unused; (5) `act-result.txt` artifact missing; (6) no evidence of actionlint validation run. Task requires TDD (tests first), Pester execution, and act validation—none demonstrated. PowerShell functions are modular, readable, and handle edge cases well, but untestable without test suite. Incomplete work.
